scripts.proof:v1: pnpm run verify && tsx scripts/v1-proof.ts && node scripts/readback-v1-proof.mjs
scripts.proof:asset-load: pnpm run verify && tsx scripts/asset-load-proof.ts && node scripts/readback-asset-load-proof.mjs
scripts.proof:entity-browser: pnpm run verify && tsx scripts/entity-browser-proof.ts && node scripts/readback-entity-browser-proof.mjs
scripts.proof:gizmo: pnpm run verify && tsx scripts/transform-gizmo-proof.ts && node scripts/readback-transform-gizmo-proof.mjs
scripts.proof:agora-compositor: pnpm run build && tsx scripts/agora-compositor-capture.ts && node scripts/readback-agora-compositor-capture.mjs
scripts.proof:browser: pnpm run proof:v1 && tsx scripts/browser-visual-capture.ts && node scripts/readback-browser-capture.mjs
scripts.proof:visual-contract: pnpm run build && tsx scripts/visual-contract-candidate.ts && node scripts/readback-visual-contract-candidate.mjs
scripts.proof:visual-capability: pnpm run proof:browser && pnpm run proof:visual-contract && tsx scripts/visual-capability-proof.ts && node scripts/readback-visual-capability-proof.mjs
scripts.verify: pnpm run check:boundaries && pnpm run test && pnpm run build && pnpm run smoke:static

knownLimitations: Task 3219 formalizes Agora compositor capture as an optional Studio visual proof backend: src/agora-compositor-capture.ts is the pure validation model (presence/blank/identity/freshness/correlation/non-claim diagnostics) and scripts/agora-compositor-capture.ts is the live harness that serves the built dist, launches it as a real webview surface under the Wayfire/Agora compositor, lets the launched surface settle, and captures the compositor surface via compositorctl. The agora_compositor_capture_proof classifies its path explicitly (captureBackend agora_compositor, captureMode compositor_surface, compositorCaptureBackend plugin_readback), verifies a nonblank frame via BOTH the compositor visual_inspection status and an independent ImageMagick metric, checks surface identity/dimensions and timestamp-based frame freshness, and correlates the frame (stamped asha_command_sequence_id) with the studio render.capture timeline sequence and the optional studio_visual_capability_proof. frameCountAtCapture/frame_count is informational only because WebKitGTK webview surfaces can render visible content without incrementing it. It preserves not_hardware_gpu/not_performance_evidence/not_native_runtime/not_wasm_authority non-claims (a compositor frame is not GPU/performance evidence) and fails closed with classified negative smokes on missing/blank/wrong/stale capture and uncorrelated timeline. It is an environment-gated proof (requires a live compositor) and is NOT part of pnpm run verify; the visual capability proof references it as an optional, additive backend and never requires it. Verified by pnpm run proof:agora-compositor.

Task 3218 adds the ASHA Studio selection/transform gizmo manipulation proof: src/transform-gizmo.ts projects the shared scene-view selected entity into a transform gizmo read model (selection, three axis handles with one command-wired active axis, and a single-axis translate with before/preview/after evidence). The translate previews editor-locally and commits through the new public transform.translate_entity command on the shared GUI/agent timeline (preview + apply, distinct sequences); the viewport 3D readback (buildStudioViewport3dReadback gizmo arg) and host render the selection outline, axis handles, preview ghost, applied box, and a transform_gizmo readback with translationBefore/After and gizmo-translate markers. The gizmo fails closed with classified diagnostics on missing_selected_entity, stale_gizmo_selection, missing_gizmo_handle, transform_readback_mismatch, and private_mutation_path. Only single-axis translate is supported in this slice; rotate/scale and multi-axis remain deferred, and it makes no physics/native runtime/Agora/GPU/performance claims (authoritative transform mutation stays behind the runtime-bridge readiness gate). Verified by pnpm run proof:gizmo.

Task 3217 adds the ASHA Studio selected entity inspector read/edit surface: src/selected-entity-inspector.ts projects the shared scene-view selected entity into identity/provenance/transform readback plus a typed field list, and adds one editable field (name) routed through the new public entity.set_name command on the shared GUI/agent timeline. The applied display name updates the viewport 3D readback (selectedEntityName + selected-entity-name marker) and is exported in the agent readout. The inspector fails closed with classified diagnostics on missing_selected_entity, inspector_readback_drift, stale_inspector_state, unsupported_field_edit, and edit_command_mismatch; transform/material/provenance fields are read-only browser/reference readback and make no Rust/WASM/native/Agora/GPU/performance claims. Verified by pnpm run proof:inspector.

Task 3216 adds the ASHA Studio entity browser / hierarchy selection sync: src/entity-browser.ts projects StudioSceneViewModel renderables into a deterministic entity list (entity ids, labels, asset/material/source-state badges, selected state), syncs hierarchy selection to the viewport selected renderable through the public selection.set_active_entity command on the shared timeline, exports readback in the agent readout, and fails closed with classified diagnostics on hierarchy/readback drift, missing selected entity, stale entity-list hash, or unsupported/private entity sources. The browser is a projection, not a private ECS or product asset DB, and makes no Rust/WASM/native/Agora/GPU/performance claims.

Task 3215 adds a real demo asset loading path through the public scene.load_asset command: a demo asset package (Catalog/AssetLock/FlatSceneDocument over @asha/contracts), a loader that emits a retained-mode render diff and named renderable ids, viewport projection of the loaded asset, structured provenance readback, and fail-closed negative smokes (missing/unsupported/material-mismatch/stale-lock-drift). Placement is browser/reference render-diff projection only and does not claim Rust/WASM authority, native runtime, Agora compositor, hardware GPU, or performance evidence; refresh proof:browser/proof:visual-capability in a Chromium environment to recapture the viewport with the loaded asset.

Task 3047 defines the runtime bridge readiness gate in docs/runtime-bridge-readiness-gate.md and src/runtime-bridge-readiness.ts; native/WASM authority remains fail-closed until @asha/runtime-bridge is an approved Studio public surface with runtime-bridge.v0 compatibility and runtime proof obligations.

Task 3046 consolidates scene/readback, pick, visual-delta crop, command/hash correlation, deployed visual-contract layout evidence, and non-claim guardrails into one agent-facing visual capability proof; it remains browser/reference/layout evidence only and does not claim Rust/WASM authority, native runtime, Agora compositor, hardware GPU, or performance evidence.

Task 3123 adds ASHA visual-contract candidate markers and proof generation for browser layout/affordance evidence only; visual-contract passes do not claim Rust/WASM authority, native runtime, Agora compositor, hardware GPU, or performance evidence.

Task 3045 adds viewport visual delta/crop proof with same-region edit-anchor crops sourced from distinct before/after viewport phase screenshots, before/after scene and crop hashes, linked command/source-state IDs, and stale-readback fail-closed guards.

Task 3044 adds viewport pick/hit-test evidence with positive selected-target hit readback, structured background no-hit evidence, timeline/selection cross-checks, and camera/viewport stale-readback fail-closed guards.
Task 3043 persists deterministic camera/tool interaction proof into StudioSceneViewModel, the shared command timeline, browser readback, and stale-readback fail-closed guards.

Task 3042 adds a Three.js-backed central browser canvas as a local projection dependency over StudioSceneViewModel; it does not import @asha/renderer-three, own authority, or claim native runtime/Agora/GPU/performance evidence.
Task 2918 provides a Studio-owned viewport editor panel that projects selected target, preview-vs-applied state, timeline correlation, and evidence refs into both UI and agent readout artifacts.

Task 2737 provides Studio-owned command batch transaction metadata, per-command batch results, partial-failure classification, and a typed V1 voxel inverse setVoxel revert workflow over public command evidence.

Task 2736 provides a public-contract model/material preview artifact using @asha/contracts StaticMeshAsset/MaterialProjection/RenderFrameDiff DTOs; upstream task 2895 promoted first-class command-registry/runtime preview surfaces, while direct Studio runtime transport consumption remains deferred.
Task 2738 provides `pnpm run proof:v1` as the end-to-end Studio V1 proof command with software_snapshot functional visual evidence.

Task 2739 provides `pnpm run proof:browser` as a Chromium headless browser screenshot capture/readback path for the Studio app and V1 proof routes; it does not claim Agora/hardware GPU/performance evidence.
@asha/studio-evidence remains deferred; current proof uses Studio-owned review/proof artifact schemas as reference evidence until that package lands.
